edges.description: Without the activator cofactor, GM2 ganglioside cannot be degraded and accumulates.
edges.target: Neuronal GM2 Ganglioside Accumulation and Neurodegeneration

nodes.description: Biallelic GM2A variants eliminate the GM2 activator protein (GM2AP), a non-enzymatic lysosomal cofactor essential for hexosaminidase A to access and hydrolyze GM2 ganglioside. The defect is mechanistically distinct from Tay-Sachs (HEXA) and Sandhoff (HEXB) disease, and enzyme activities are normal.

evidence.reference_title: GM2 gangliosidosis AB variant: first case of late onset and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: two mutations in the GM2A gene encoding the GM2 activator protein (GM2-AP), an essential co-factor of hexosaminidase A

meta.association: Biallelic loss-of-function GM2A variants causing GM2 activator protein deficiency

evidence.reference_title: GM2 gangliosidosis AB variant: first case of late onset and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: two mutations in the GM2A gene encoding the GM2 activator protein (GM2-AP), an essential co-factor of hexosaminidase A

nodes.description: GM2 ganglioside accumulates in neuronal lysosomes because hexosaminidase A cannot degrade it without the activator, driving progressive neurodegeneration.
nodes.id: Neuronal GM2 Ganglioside Accumulation and Neurodegeneration

evidence.reference_title: GM2 gangliosidosis AB variant: first case of late onset and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: neurodegenerative diseases caused by lysosomal accumulation of GM2 gangliosides
